license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bipikdke
title: SARS-CoV-2 Tailed Amplicon Illumina Sequencing
author: Daryl Gohl

steps_raw.instruction: Clean workspace and pipets by spraying with RNaseZAP or comparable product (such as RNase Away) and wiping down with KimWipes prior to beginning work. RNA samples should be stored at -80 °C and thawed on ice.
Thaw RNA samples on ice then transfer 5 µL of sample into a 96-well Thermo PCR plate.
Set up the following reverse transcription reaction master mix (multiply below volumes by number of reactions plus desired overage): 11 µL, nuclease free water 4 µL, SuperScript IV VILO master mix
Transfer 15 µL of reverse transcription master mix to each sample containing well.
Seal plate with a “B” seal, mix well by vortexing using a plate vortexer at 1900 rpm for 00:00:10 s, and spin down briefly in a plate centrifuge ( 00:00:05 s at 2500 rpm, °C, .
Incubate in a thermocycler using the following conditions: 25 °C for 00:10:00 50 °C for 00:10:00 85 °C for 00:05:00
Transfer 2.5 µL of cDNA to each of 4 96-well Thermo PCR plates labeled: Project_Name_PCR1_1.1, Project_Name_PCR1_1.2, Project_Name_PCR1_2.1, and Project_Name_PCR1_2.2.

Set up the following four PCR master mixes, one for each of the four multiplexed primer pools (multiply below volumes by number of reactions plus desired overage): 14.75 µL, nuclease-free water 5 µL, 5x Q5 reaction buffer 0.5 µL, 10mM dNTPs 0.25 µL, Q5 Polymerase 2 µL, primer pool (10 µM) (Either pool 1.1, 1.2, 2.1, or 2.2)
Transfer 22.5 µL of master mix to each well of the appropriate PCR plate.
Seal plate with a “B” seal, mix well by vortexing using a plate vortexer at 1900 rpm for 00:00:10 , and spin down briefly in a plate centrifuge ( 00:00:05 at 2500 rpm, °C, ).
Amplify samples using the following PCR conditions: 98 °C for 00:00:30 35 cycles of: 98 °C for 00:00:15 65 °C for 00:05:00
For each sample, combine 10 µL of each of the four pools in a single Bio-Rad fully-skirted 96 well plate.
Seal plate with a “F” seal, mix well by vortexing using a plate vortexer at 1900 rpm for 00:00:10 , and spin down in a plate centrifuge ( 00:00:30 at 2500 rpm, °C, ).
In a 96-well Thermo plate, add 2 µL of each sample to 198 µL of nuclease free water (1:100 dilution).
Seal plate with a “F” seal, mix well by vortexing using a plate vortexer at 2500 rpm for 00:00:10 , and spin down in a plate centrifuge ( 00:00:30 at 2500 rpm, °C, ).
Transfer 5 µL of 1:100 diluted PCR 1 sample to a 96-well Thermo PCR plate.
Transfer 2 µL of 5 µM indexing primer mix to the 96-well Thermo PCR plate containing the samples.

Set up the following PCR master mix (multiply below volumes by number of reactions plus desired overage): 0.7 µL, nuclease-free water 2 µL, 5x Q5 reaction buffer 0.2 µL, 10 mM dNTPs 0.1 µL, Q5 Polymerase
Transfer 3 µL of master mix to each well of the appropriate PCR plate.
Seal plate with a “B” seal, mix well by vortexing using a plate vortexer at 1900 rpm for 00:00:10 , and spin down briefly in a plate centrifuge ( 00:00:05 at 2500 rpm, °C, ).
Amplify samples using the following PCR conditions: 98 °C for 00:00:30 10 cycles of: 98 °C for 00:00:20 55 °C for 00:00:15 72 °C for 00:01:00 72 °C for 00:05:00
Normalize samples using a SequalPrep plate according to manufacturer’s instructions. sequalprep_platekit_man.pdf
Elute in 20 µL of SequalPrep Elution Buffer.
Pool 10 µL of each sample in a trough, mix well and transfer material to a 1.5 mL non-stick tube.
Purify using AMPureXP beads at a 0.7x ratio. Elute library in 20 µL of EB.

Perform final QC on pool by determining concentration (PicoGreen or Qubit assay). Prepare 2 nM pool dilution, based on the sample concentration as determined by PicoGreen and fragment size (expected size is ~555 bp).
Dilute pooled sample to 8 pM in HT1, following MiSeq loading instructions, spike in 5% 8 pM PhiX, and load in MiSeq 2x250 or 2x300 reagent cartridge.
